title: STEPHEN ALDEHYDE SYNTHESIS (STEPHEN REDUCTION)
summary: Overview page for the Stephen aldehyde synthesis (Stephen reduction) describing reduction of aliphatic and aromatic nitriles with hydrogen chloride and stannous chloride to imidoyl chlorides/aldimine complexes that hydrolyze to aldehydes.
notes: Key concepts: nitrile activation by HCl, reduction with SnCl2, formation of imidoyl chloride or aldimine hexachlorostannane intermediates, and hydrolysis to aldehydes under warm aqueous workup.

title: STEPHEN ALDEHYDE SYNTHESIS (STEPHEN REDUCTION)
summary: Application page for the Stephen reduction featuring a pyrazolo[3,4-b][1,8]naphthyridine derivative, [1,1'-binaphthalenyl]-2,2'-dicarbaldehyde, tripodal polypeptides, and trans-stilbene benzenesulfonamide derivatives, showing nitrile-to-aldehyde conversion in heteroaromatic, biaryl, peptide-template, and medicinal-chemistry settings.
notes: Examples emphasize modified Stephen conditions on aromatic nitriles, cyanation/reduction sequences, TREN-based template synthesis, and Raney nickel alloy/formic acid reduction as a practical variant.

title: STETTER REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Stetter reaction describing cyanide- or thiazolium-catalyzed umpolung addition of aldehydes to Michael acceptors such as enones, enoates, and activated nitriles to furnish 1,4-dicarbonyl and related acyl anion equivalent products.
notes: Key concepts: reversible benzoin-type generation of an acyl anion equivalent, irreversible conjugate addition to activated alkenes, use of NaCN/KCN or thiazolium salts, and access to γ-diketones, γ-oxo nitriles, or 1,4,7-triketones.

title: STETTER REACTION
summary: Application page for the Stetter reaction featuring CI-981, roseophilin, trans-sabinene hydrate, and (+)-monomorine I, highlighting catalytic intermolecular and intramolecular acyl-anion conjugate additions in natural-product and medicinal-target synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize chiral thiazolium catalysis, Paal-Knorr pyrrole precursor formation, tandem Stetter/intramolecular aldol strategies, and masked enedione construction en route to alkaloid frameworks.

title: STEVENS REARRANGEMENT
summary: Overview page for the Stevens rearrangement describing base-promoted [1,2]-migration of ammonium or sulfonium ylides generated from quaternary onium salts to give rearranged tertiary amines or sulfides.
notes: Key concepts: formation of nitrogen or sulfur ylides from quaternary salts, migratory aptitude effects, radical-pair or concerted pathways, and distinct behavior of ammonium versus sulfonium substrates under basic conditions.

title: STEVENS REARRANGEMENT
summary: Application page for the Stevens rearrangement featuring a 5,7-fused isoindolo-benzazepine system, (+)-desoxycodeine-D, 1,2-(1,1'-ferrocenediyl)ethene, and (P)-(+)-pentahelicene, showing ylide rearrangement in alkaloid, organometallic, and helicene synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize transition-metal-catalyzed diazo-ester ylide generation, quaternary ammonium salt rearrangement with phenyllithium, sulfonium-salt alternatives, and axial-to-central chirality transfer in helicene synthesis.
